snippets.title: Keras EarlyStopping with patience and restore best weights
snippets.code:
from tensorflow.keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping

early_stop = EarlyStopping(
    monitor="val_loss",
    patience=10,
    restore_best_weights=True,
    mode="min",
)

history = model.fit(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    epochs=100,
    callbacks=[early_stop],
)

print("Stopped at epoch:", len(history.history["loss"]))
print("Best val_loss:", min(history.history["val_loss"]))
snippets.explanation: monitors val_loss each epoch; stops after 10 epochs without improvement and reloads weights from the best validation epoch automatically.

snippets.title: Manual PyTorch loop with best checkpoint restore
snippets.code:
import copy

best_val_loss = float("inf")
best_state = None
patience, wait = 10, 0

for epoch in range(100):
    train_one_epoch(model, train_loader)
    val_loss = evaluate(model, val_loader)

    if val_loss < best_val_loss:
        best_val_loss = val_loss
        best_state = copy.deepcopy(model.state_dict())
        wait = 0
    else:
        wait += 1
        if wait >= patience:
            break

model.load_state_dict(best_state)  # restore best weights
snippets.explanation: Tracks the best validation loss, saves state_dict at each improvement, stops after patience exhausted, then loads the best checkpoint — not the final epoch.

snippets.title: XGBoost early_stopping_rounds and best_iteration
snippets.code:
import xgboost as xgb

clf = xgb.XGBClassifier(
    n_estimators=500,
    learning_rate=0.05,
    eval_metric="logloss",
    early_stopping_rounds=20,
)

clf.fit(
    X_train, y_train,
    eval_set=[(X_val, y_val)],
    verbose=False,
)

print("best_iteration:", clf.best_iteration)
y_pred = clf.predict(X_test, iteration_range=(0, clf.best_iteration + 1))
snippets.explanation: Patience appears as early_stopping_rounds. Use best_iteration for prediction so late overfitting trees are excluded.

snippets.title: sklearn warm_start with validation monitoring
snippets.code:
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import log_loss

clf = MLPClassifier(hidden_layer_sizes=(64, 32), max_iter=1, warm_start=True)
best_loss, best_coefs, best_intercepts = float("inf"), None, None
patience, wait = 5, 0

for epoch in range(200):
    clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
    val_loss = log_loss(y_val, clf.predict_proba(X_val))
    if val_loss < best_loss:
        best_loss, wait = val_loss, 0
        best_coefs = [w.copy() for w in clf.coefs_]
        best_intercepts = [b.copy() for b in clf.intercepts_]
    else:
        wait += 1
        if wait >= patience:
            break

clf.coefs_, clf.intercepts_ = best_coefs, best_intercepts
snippets.explanation: When a library lacks built-in early stopping, warm_start enables epoch loops with manual patience and explicit restore of best coefficients.
